text: Last class you presented your ideas with a clear structure.
Today you learn to bargain on price and terms with conditions.

If you increase the volume, I will lower the price.
Unless you confirm the date, I cannot offer a discount.
Provided you pay on time, we will give a small discount.
If we cannot agree, I will walk away.
Your price is a little high. Unless you lower it, we cannot sign this week.
I hear you. If you increase the volume, I will give you a small discount. That is a fair trade-off.
We have some leeway on the volume. But a late shipment is a deal-breaker for us.
Provided you confirm the order today, I will guarantee delivery by the tenth.
That works. Let us meet halfway: a two percent discount for a bigger order, delivered on time.
Agreed. We have a deal. Thank you for negotiating in good faith.
If you increase the volume, I will give you a small discount.

My bottom line is the floor price; that is a fair trade-off.
Let us meet halfway and close the deal in good faith.

Hello Walyson, this is Clara from the buying team. I am calling about the Santos coffee contract. We are interested, but the price is a little high for us right now. Here is our position. If you can lower the price by two percent, we will sign this week. If you keep the current price, we will need more time to decide. We do have some leeway on the volume, so provided you give us a small discount, we can increase our order. One thing is firm: we cannot accept a late shipment, so a delivery after the tenth is a deal-breaker for us. Please call me back with a ballpark number, and let us try to meet halfway. We want to work in good faith and close this deal.

This is my preparation note for the negotiation with Clara. My goal is a fair deal for both sides. First, I know my bottom line: I cannot go below the floor price from the mill. My leverage is the strong harvest and the high quality from Santos. If they ask for a big discount, I will not just say yes. Instead, I will offer a trade-off: a small discount provided they increase the volume. Unless they accept a firm delivery date, I will not lower the price. If we cannot agree, I am ready to walk away, but I really want to meet halfway. I will give them a ballpark number first, then we negotiate the final terms in good faith.

First, prepare your bottom line and know your leverage before the meeting. Next, open in good faith and give the other side a ballpark number. After that, listen to their position and look for a trade-off you can both accept. Then, if they push for more, offer a small concession provided they give you something in return. Finally, meet halfway and agree on the final terms, or walk away if the deal does not work.
